title: schismus barbatus subsp. calycinus

parts: Schismus barbatus subsp. calycinus has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and inflorescences. The leaves of Schismus barbatus subsp. calycinus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant contain saponins and glycosides, which are reported to have antimicrobial and antihelmintic activities. preparations: Schismus barbatus subsp. calycinus has many herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, that are used for medicinal purposes. The tincture, on the other hand, is typically used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. side_effects: Schismus barbatus subsp. calycinus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to the high concentration of toxic compounds like alkaloids and saponins.
